title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Eczema
intro: Herbal teas for Eczema are natural remedies made from plants and herbs that are used to treat and soothe the symptoms of eczema, a chronic skin condition characterized by dry, itchy, and inflamed skin. These herbal teas can effectively treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and moisturizing properties, which help to reduce redness, itching, and irritation. Examples of herbal teas used to treat eczema include chamomile tea, which calms and soothes the skin, peppermint tea, which cools and reduces inflammation, calendula tea, which promotes healing and reduces itching, and lavender tea, which reduces stress and promotes relaxation. Other herbal teas, such as ginger tea, green tea, and licorice root tea, also have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate eczema symptoms, making them a popular and natural alternative to traditional treatments.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema. The bioactive constituents of Calendula officinalis teas, including triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development and progression of eczema. By using Calendula officinalis teas topically, individuals with eczema may experience improved skin health, reduced symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Calendula officinalis flowers and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Calendula officinalis flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the flowers. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of Calendula officinalis tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Forschende Komplementarmedizin", Calendula officinalis teas for eczema may be of value in selected dermatological indications.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used as a potential natural remedy for eczema, but some possible side effects include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your skin condition closely, and avoid using Calendula officinalis teas on broken or sensitive skin, as well as in areas exposed to direct sunlight.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of dried Calendula flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply to affected skin areas.
Use this tea compress 2-3 times a day for best results.
Combine with a gentle skincare routine for optimal eczema relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lectins, help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from eczema symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, Urtica dioica teas can help to treat eczema by preventing the formation of new lesions and reducing the severity of existing ones. The benefits of using Urtica dioica teas to treat eczema include reduced itching, improved skin texture, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of eczema flare-ups.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Urtica dioica leaves.
Combine the Urtica dioica leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into another cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, for eczema relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Urtica dioica teas for eczema may be beneficial as an extract of Urtica dioica has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing and potentially help alleviate eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat eczema due to its anti-inflammatory properties, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches in some individuals. To use Urtica dioica teas safely, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with a low dose, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding it if you have allergies or are pregnant/breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Urtica dioica tea for eczema treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Urtica dioica tea, 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2-3 teaspoons per cup over time.
Use Urtica dioica tea for 4-6 weeks to see noticeable improvements.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat eczema, an inflammatory skin condition characterized by redness, itching, and dryness. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of aloe vera teas help to calm and reduce the symptoms of eczema, promoting skin hydration and healing. The bioactive constituents, including vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids and anthraquinones, work together to reduce inflammation, prevent water loss, and promote the regeneration of skin cells. The benefits of aloe vera teas for eczema treatment include reduced itching and redness, improved skin hydration, and accelerated wound healing, making it a popular and effective herbal remedy for this common skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe vera gel, 2 cups of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of honey, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the Aloe vera gel in the tea infuser or strainer.
Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Add the honey to the liquid and stir well to dissolve.
Drink the Aloe vera tea immediately, up to 3 times a day, as needed for eczema relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Forschende Komplementarmedizin", Aloe vera teas for eczema may be of value in selected dermatological indications.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can be beneficial in treating eczema, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions, especially in individuals who are sensitive to aloe vera. To use Aloe vera teas safely, it's essential to start with small doses, monitor your body's response, and avoid taking it if you experience any discomfort or irritation, and also be cautious when consuming it if you have any underlying digestive issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Aloe vera tea 2 times a day
Use 1-2 tablespoons of Aloe vera gel on affected skin
Apply gel 2-3 times a day or as needed
Leave gel on for 15-30 minutes before rinsing off
Continue treatment for 2-4 weeks or as directed by doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, derived from the oat plant, have been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including eczema. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with eczema, promoting a calm and healthy skin environment. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa, including avenanthramides and beta-glucans, work together to modulate the immune response and reduce inflammation, providing relief from eczema symptoms. By incorporating Avena sativa teas into a treatment regimen, individuals may experience benefits such as reduced skin irritation, improved skin elasticity, and a decrease in eczema flare-ups.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the Avena sativa in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of honey (optional) to the Avena sativa tea for sweetness.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used as a potential natural remedy for eczema, but its use may be associated with side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with small doses, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming Avena sativa teas in large quantities, especially before driving or engaging in activities that require alertness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Avena sativa tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried oat straw per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain before drinking
Continue usage for 4-6 weeks to see noticeable improvement
Adjust dosage to 2 tablespoons per cup for severe eczema cases

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, such as alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation, soothe the skin, and promote wound healing, making it an effective remedy for eczema. Echinacea purpurea teas work by reducing the severity of eczema symptoms, such as redness, itching, and swelling, and promoting the growth of new skin cells to replace damaged ones. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of eczema outbreaks.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 2 tablespoons of honey (optional) and have it ready for later use.
Pour the boiling water over the Echinacea flowers in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Add honey to taste, and your Echinacea tea for eczema is ready to drink.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used as a potential treatment for eczema, but its use may lead to side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. When using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat eczema, it's essential to take precautions by monitoring your body's response, avoiding excessive consumption, and being cautious of potential interactions with other herbal preparations or supplements.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea purpurea tea for eczema treatment.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 1-2 cups daily.
Use Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a week for maintenance, 3-4 times for flare-ups.
Monitor skin condition and adjust tea usage as advised by your doctor or healthcare provider.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, including rosmarinic acid and eugenol, help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with eczema. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Melissa officinalis teas can also help to alleviate the emotional symptoms often associated with eczema, such as anxiety and depression. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of eczema flare-ups.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups a day to help with eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat eczema due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, but possible side effects include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications such as blood thinners. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to use high-quality Melissa officinalis teas, monitor your skin for any adverse reactions, and avoid combining it with other herbal preparations, especially those that thin the blood or exacerbate eczema symptoms.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 tablespoon of Melissa officinalis in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Use 1-2 tablespoons of Melissa officinalis tea as a compress, cooled, on affected areas.
Apply the compress for 15-30 minutes, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Continue usage for 2-4 weeks to observe improvement in eczema symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been used in traditional medicine to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These teas contain bioactive constituents such as silymarin and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation, promote skin healing, and protect the skin from oxidative damage. By consuming Silybum marianum teas, individuals can potentially alleviate symptoms of eczema, such as redness, itching, and skin dryness, and promote overall skin health. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin hydration, and enhanced immune function, making it a potential natural remedy for this chronic condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Put the Silybum marianum flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for relief from eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat eczema due to its anti-inflammatory properties, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications. When using Silybum marianum teas to treat eczema, precautions should be taken to avoid overuse, as excessive consumption can lead to liver damage, and it should also be used with caution during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as there may be risks associated with its use in these situations.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea, 2 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Use Silybum marianum tea for 3-6 months to see noticeable results.
Combine Silybum marianum tea with other eczema treatments as advised by a doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort tea, have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat eczema by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, thereby alleviating symptoms such as itching, redness, and skin lesions. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum perforatum, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects, particularly the flavonoids hyperoside and rutin, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin regeneration, and enhanced immune function, making it a promising natural remedy for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Hypericum perforatum leaves.
Use 1 tablespoon of the leaves and chop them finely.
Steep the chopped leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves. Add honey to taste, if needed.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC veterinary research", Hypericum perforatum teas for eczema may be beneficial due to its antibacterial and antifungal effects and its ability to promote wound healing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat eczema, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, which can interact with other medications and exacerbate certain health conditions. To use Hypericum perforatum teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, gradually increasing the amount as needed, and to avoid consuming it with other herbal preparations or medications, especially blood thinners and antidepressants.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Hypericum perforatum tea 2-3 times daily
Use tea as a warm compress on affected eczema areas
Apply tea directly to eczema patches 2-3 times daily
Mix 1 tablespoon of tea with bath water for a soothing soak
Consult a doctor for proper dosage and treatment plan

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The teas contain bioactive constituents such as quinine and alkaloids, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema. These properties help to treat eczema by soothing the skin, reducing redness and irritation, and promoting healing. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas for eczema treatment include reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of eczema flare-ups.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, a cup of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot until it reaches a boil, then reduce the heat.
Add the dried Cinchona officinalis bark to the tea infuser or strainer, and put it in the pot.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer.
Let the tea cool down, then drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for eczema relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat eczema due to its anti-inflammatory properties; however, possible side effects may include stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications that affect blood clotting, such as warfarin.

Precautions to take when using Cinchona officinalis teas for eczema treatment include avoiding excessive consumption, as it may lead to caffeine overload, and being aware of any sensitivity or allergic reactions, especially when combining it with other herbal remedies or medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Cinchona officinalis for eczema treatment.
Take 1 cup of boiled water and add 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona bark
Steep for 5-7 minutes and let the mixture cool down
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, 2-3 times a week
Monitor eczema symptoms and adjust dosage as advised by your doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, thereby reducing redness and itching associated with eczema. These constituents also have been shown to promote skin barrier function and modulate the immune response, thereby reducing the severity of eczema symptoms. The benefits of using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin hydration, and enhanced skin elasticity, making it a promising natural remedy for this chronic skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers, or 2 teaspoons of dried ones.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the boiling water to help soothe skin.
Add the Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers (or dried ones) to the boiling water, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it warm, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve eczema symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema, thereby providing relief to affected individuals. The anti-inflammatory properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help to calm the skin, reduce redness, and promote healing, ultimately leading to the improvement of eczema symptoms. Regular consumption of this herbal tea has been found to provide benefits in treating eczema, including reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Clean the roots and chop them into small pieces.
Use 1 cup of boiling water and add 2 tablespoons of chopped roots to a tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally 2 hours before bedtime.
preparations.preparation_study: According to American journal of clinical dermatology, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas do not have specific evidence for the treatment of eczema, but it is mentioned as one of the plants that appear promising for atopic dermatitis, which is a related inflammatory skin disease.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat eczema due to its anti-inflammatory properties, but possible side effects may include increased blood pressure, sodium retention, and potassium deficiency, which can lead to symptoms such as headaches, muscle weakness, and fatigue. To minimize these risks, it is essential to monitor your blood pressure and electrolyte levels closely, drink plenty of water to stay hydrated, and avoid using these teas excessively or for extended periods.

Aloe barbadensis teas help to treat eczema by reducing the severity of symptoms, such as redness, itching, and dryness, and promoting a healthier skin barrier function. Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.

The tea's bioactive constituents, including silica and flavonoids, help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema. By promoting skin hydration and elasticity, Equisetum arvense teas can help to improve the overall health and appearance of the skin, making it an effective treatment for eczema. The benefits of using horsetail tea to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin barrier function, and a decrease in the severity and frequency of eczema outbreaks.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Equisetum arvense leaves and stems. Clean them with water.
Put the cleaned Equisetum arvense in a saucepan and add 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid.
Mix 1 tablespoon of honey with 1 cup of the strained Equisetum arvense tea. Stir well.
Drink the Equisetum arvense tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be used as a potential treatment for eczema, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your skin for any signs of irritation, and avoid using the teas if you experience any adverse reactions, and also be cautious of interactions with other medications you may be taking.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat eczema, an inflammatory skin condition characterized by dry, itchy, and scaly patches. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba teas help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of eczema, promoting skin health and hydration. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits in treating eczema. By using Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals with eczema may experience improved skin condition, reduced itching and irritation, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to PharmacoEconomics, Ginkgo biloba teas for eczema have not been shown to have sufficient high-quality evidence to support their use, with limited information available on their costs, efficacy, and safety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate eczema symptoms, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as itching, redness, and rashes. Precautions to take when using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat eczema include being cautious with the dosage, starting with small amounts to monitor tolerance, and avoiding consumption if you have bleeding disorders or are taking anticoagulant medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties. The herbal preparation helps to calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing by enhancing skin hydration and improving skin elasticity. The bioactive constituents, including asiatic acid, madecassic acid, and centelloside, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that contribute to the treatment of eczema. Regular consumption of Centella asiatica teas may help to alleviate eczema symptoms, promote skin health, and reduce the risk of skin infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Centella asiatica leaves and flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Centella asiatica mixture to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink immediately.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Metabolites, Centella asiatica teas for eczema may be beneficial due to their ability to promote fibroblast proliferation and collagen synthesis, which can aid in wound repair and potentially reduce symptoms of eczema.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Centella asiatica teas can be beneficial in treating eczema, but potential side effects may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners. To use Centella asiatica teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming it if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergies, as well as be cautious when combining it with other herbal remedies.

preparations.herb_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus teas, also known as blueberry leaf tea, have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The anthocyanins and flavonoids present in this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By reducing the severity of eczema symptoms, Vaccinium myrtillus teas can provide relief from itching, redness, and skin irritation. The benefits of using Vaccinium myrtillus teas to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of eczema outbreaks.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Vaccinium myrtillus leaves or 1 cup of dried leaves.
Rinse the leaves with water and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the leaves with 4 cups of water in a pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium myrtillus teas can be beneficial in treating eczema due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, but potential side effects include gastrointestinal issues, such as stomach upset and diarrhea, and allergic reactions, including hives and itching. To use Vaccinium myrtillus teas safely, individuals should start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid taking it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and monitor their body's response, as some people may experience more severe side effects, such as kidney damage or interactions with medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Chamomilla recutita teas have been traditionally used to treat eczema due to their anti-inflammatory, soothing, and calming properties that help to reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with this ailment. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that contribute to its therapeutic benefits. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, Chamomilla recutita teas help to alleviate the symptoms of eczema, promoting a more comfortable and manageable condition. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat eczema include reduced itching and inflammation, improved skin hydration, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Chamomilla recutita flowers and 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of Chamomilla recutita flowers to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a separate cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, to make the tea more palatable.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Chamomilla recutita teas are not specifically mentioned for eczema treatment in the provided study, but German chamomile flower, which is likely referring to Chamomilla recutita, is listed among other CAM agents associated with significant adverse effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Chamomilla recutita teas can be used as a potential treatment for eczema, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and dizziness in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Chamomilla recutita teas to treat eczema, including using it in moderation, avoiding it during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications.

preparation_study: According to Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, Calendula officinalis teas for eczema may have anti-inflammatory effects and promote wound healing, as part of a three-herb formula with Cortex Moutan and Herba Menthae, significantly reducing symptoms of atopic dermatitis such as ear swelling and epidermal thickness in an oxazolone-induced AD mice model.
intro_study: According to "Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie", teas for eczema may not be explicitly mentioned, but the study suggests that natural herbal medicines, including some that are consumed as teas, have shown remarkable potential in treating atopic dermatitis (AD), a condition related to eczema.
